objectives.title: Database & API Layer — enable multi-user platform mode
objectives.description: Transform Forge from single-user CLI tool to a multi-client platform by implementing PostgreSQL storage adapter and FastAPI REST API. This enables the Web UI (O-002), real-time collaboration, and knowledge versioning with diff support. The CLI remains fully functional in standalone JSON mode — platform mode is additive, not replacement.

key_results.metric: REST API endpoints implemented (from spec)
PostgreSQL adapter passing feature parity tests
JSON → PostgreSQL migration tool working
WebSocket event types implemented

assumptions: PostgreSQL 16 + Redis 7 available via Docker Compose
FastAPI + SQLAlchemy is the right Python web stack for this
LiteLLM can be deferred — direct provider adapters first
Event sourcing via Redis Streams is worth the complexity for audit trail

objectives.title: Web UI — visual entity management with drag & drop
objectives.description: Build a React/Next.js frontend providing visual management of all Forge entities. Drag & drop enables intuitive relationship building (knowledge→task, guideline→objective, lesson→knowledge promotion). Real-time updates via WebSocket keep the UI in sync with CLI and API operations. This is the primary user-facing value of the platform mode.

key_results.metric: Entity CRUD pages implemented (objectives, ideas, tasks, knowledge, guidelines, AC templates, decisions, lessons)
Drag & drop interactions working (from DnD matrix)
Real-time WebSocket updates working
Task DAG visualization rendered

assumptions: Next.js 14 App Router is stable enough for production
DnD Kit handles our entity-to-entity drag patterns (not just list reordering)
Zustand is sufficient for state management (no Redux needed)
API (O-001) provides all needed endpoints before UI work begins

objectives.title: AI Features — LLM-powered suggestions and execution streaming
objectives.description: Implement LLM provider adapters (Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama) and AI-powered features: knowledge suggestions, guideline matching, AC suggestions, lesson evaluation, and impact assessment. Add execution view showing real-time LLM output alongside assembled context. This is the intelligence layer that makes Forge more than a project tracker.

key_results.metric: LLM provider adapters implemented (Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama)
AI suggestion endpoints working
LLM contracts defined (from registry spec)
Execution streaming view (context + LLM output side-by-side)

assumptions: LiteLLM as transport layer handles provider differences adequately
AI suggestions are useful enough to justify the LLM cost
Streaming via WebSocket provides acceptable latency
Contract-based validation catches most LLM output issues

objectives.title: Platform Integration — containerization and end-to-end operation
objectives.description: Package the full platform (API + Web UI + PostgreSQL + Redis) in Docker Compose for local development and deployment. Ensure CLI works seamlessly in both standalone and remote modes via ForgeClient abstraction. Complete remaining Phase 1 items (ForgeClient, full SKILL.md migration). This is the glue that makes the platform usable as a whole.

key_results.metric: Docker Compose services running (forge-api, forge-web, postgres, redis)
CLI remote mode (APIBackend) working end-to-end
ForgeClient abstraction implemented (standalone + api modes)
JSON → PostgreSQL migration tested with real project data

assumptions: Rancher Desktop / Docker Desktop available on dev machine
Port conflicts (3000, 8000, 5432, 6379) manageable
ForgeClient can wrap existing module functions without major refactoring

objectives.title: UI/UX Planning - Kompletny interfejs wspierajacy workflow uzytkownika
objectives.description: Zaplanowac i zbudowac interfejs Forge Platform, ktory aktywnie wspiera workflow uzytkownika: od zdefiniowania celu (objective), przez eksploracje pomyslow (ideas), planowanie zadan (tasks), wykonanie z monitoringiem LLM, po wyciaganie wnioskow (lessons). Interfejs musi byc SZYBKI w obsludze - minimum klikniec, maximum informacji. Kluczowe zasady: (1) Kazda encja ma pelne CRUD z poziomu UI, (2) Powiazania miedzy encjami sa widoczne i klikalne, (3) Real-time aktualizacje przez WebSocket, (4) Dashboard jako centrum dowodzenia z quick actions, (5) Nawigacja kontekstowa - z task widac powiazane decisions/knowledge/guidelines. Stan obecny: ~30% endpointow API (72 total) ma pokrycie w UI. Brakuje: formularzy tworzenia dla 6/11 encji (tasks, decisions, objectives, ideas, lessons, knowledge), widoku szczegolowego dla tasks/decisions/ideas, aktualizacji KR, konfiguracji gates, promocji lessons, hierarchii ideas, ustawien projektu.

key_results.metric: Encje z pelnym CRUD w UI (create/read/update) z 11
Endpointy API pokryte przez UI (z 72 total)
Kompletne user flows end-to-end dzialajace bez bledu
Srednia liczba klikniec do wykonania typowej operacji

assumptions: Interfejs musi obslugiwac pelne CRUD dla: projects, tasks, decisions, objectives, ideas, knowledge, guidelines, lessons, ac-templates, gates, execution
Dashboard jako command center: aktywne cele, pipeline zadan, otwarte decyzje, recent activity, quick actions
Kazdy widok encji musi pokazywac powiazania (task->decisions, knowledge, guidelines; idea->children, KRs)
Formularze tworzenia inline lub modal - bez przechodzenia na osobna strone
Real-time aktualizacje list przez WebSocket (nowy task pojawia sie bez refresha)
Pipeline view: DAG + Kanban + filtrowanie po statusie/scope/agent
Widok szczegolowy encji z tabami: content, relations, history, context
Ustawienia projektu: gates config, test_cmd, lint_cmd, scopes
Nawigacja kontekstowa: z poziomu task widac wytyczne, wiedze, decyzje - klikalne linki
Bulk operations: oznacz wiele tasks jako DONE, przypisz scope do wielu guidelines

objectives.title: Testowanie wszystkich funkcjonalnosci - weryfikacja end-to-end przez UI
objectives.description: Kompleksowe przetestowanie WSZYSTKICH 72 endpointow API Forge Platform przez interfejs webowy. Kazdy endpoint musi byc wywolany, wynik zweryfikowany, bledy obsluzone. Obejmuje: (1) CRUD dla kazdej z 11 encji, (2) Pipeline lifecycle: create project -> add objectives -> add ideas -> plan tasks -> claim next -> execute -> complete -> record changes -> extract lessons, (3) WebSocket real-time: eventy pojawiaja sie bez refresha, (4) AI suggestions: knowledge, guidelines, AC - czy poprawnie sugeruja, (5) Debug monitor: enable -> execute -> view session -> verify captured data, (6) Edge cases: duplikaty, puste pola, concurrent access, stale data. Priorytet: znalezc i naprawic WSZYSTKIE bledy zanim uzytkownik je zobaczy.

key_results.metric: Endpointy API zweryfikowane przez UI (z 72)
Wykryte i naprawione bugi
Scenariusze E2E przechodzace bez bledu
Pokrycie edge cases (procent zdefiniowanych)

assumptions: Testowanie odbywa sie manualnie przez interfejs webowy po zbudowaniu UI
Kazdy endpoint API musi miec odpowiadajacy element UI do wywolania
WebSocket musi dostarczac real-time aktualizacje (task status, new entities)
AI suggestions musza dzialac w kontekscie - nie random dane
Debug monitor musi przechwytywac pelne sesje LLM z poprawnymi danymi
Edge cases: walidacja formularzy, duplikaty slugow, nieistniejace ID, timeout

objectives.title: Debug Console - pelny wglad w dzialanie systemu i interakcje LLM
objectives.description: Zbudowac konsole debugowania ktora daje PELNY wglad w to co dzieje sie w systemie. Nie chodzi o prosty log - chodzi o czytelne, atrakcyjne graficznie, INFORMUJACE narzedzie diagnostyczne. Trzy warstwy: (1) API Inspector - kazde wywolanie HTTP: URL, method, payload, response, status code, czas trwania, kto wyslal, (2) LLM Monitor - pelny wglad w interakcje z modelami AI: co zostalo wyslane (jaki task, jakie objectives, jakie guidelines, jaka dokladna tresc promptu), co otrzymano (pelna odpowiedz), gdzie przekazano wynik, jakie efekty wywolalo (utworzone encje, zmienione statusy), kto wyslal, do ktorego modelu, dlaczego (contract_id, task context), ile tokenow zuzyto, ile to kosztowalo, (3) Event Stream - zdarzenia WebSocket w czasie rzeczywistym z filtrowaniem po typie. Konsola musi byc ZAWSZE dostepna jako wysuwalny panel dolny z kazdego widoku, z mozliwoscia otwarcia na pelny ekran. Filtrowanie, wyszukiwanie, kolorowanie po typie/statusie, auto-scroll z mozliwoscia zatrzymania.

key_results.metric: Typy zdarzen widocznych w konsoli (API calls, LLM sessions, WS events, errors, task lifecycle, decision changes, knowledge updates, execution chunks)
Kompletnosc danych LLM session (prompt sent, context sections, response, tokens, cost, effects, who/where/why)
Czas potrzebny na zdiagnozowanie bledu (minuty)
Ocena uzytecznosci konsoli przez uzytkownika (1-10)

assumptions: Konsola dostepna jako wysuwalny panel dolny z KAZDEGO widoku (nie osobna strona)
Mozliwosc otwarcia na pelny ekran lub jako osobna zakladka
API Inspector przechwytuje WSZYSTKIE fetch() - nie wymaga zmian w backendzie
LLM Monitor wykorzystuje istniejacy Debug Capture backend (T-037, 6 endpointow)
Kazda sesja LLM pokazuje: co wyslano (prompt + context sections z nazwami), co otrzymano, efekty (jakie encje utworzono/zmieniono), koszty (tokeny in/out)
Event Stream pokazuje WS eventy z kolorowaniem: task.status_changed, decision.created, knowledge.updated, execution.output, itp.
Filtrowanie po: typie zdarzenia, statusie, task_id, czasie, severity
Wyszukiwanie pelnotekstowe w logach
Auto-scroll z mozliwoscia zatrzymania gdy uzytkownik czyta
Eksport logow do pliku JSON/CSV

objectives.title: Objectives module overhaul — descriptive KR, LLM enrichment, cross-linking
objectives.description: Current Objectives module has critical limitations: (1) Key Results require hard numeric metrics (metric+baseline+target) but real objectives often need descriptive success criteria — 'system handles 10k concurrent users' is better expressed as a description than forced into a number. (2) No LLM enrichment during creation — user must manually craft KRs without AI assistance. Skills (deep-explore, deep-verify) should be invocable during objective creation to help draft better KRs and validate assumptions. (3) Guidelines are only assignable via scopes, not individually — user should be able to attach specific guideline IDs to an objective. (4) Knowledge objects should be linkable to objectives (knowledge_ids field exists in backend but has no UI). (5) No support for dependencies between objectives — some objectives naturally depend on others (e.g., 'Build UI' depends on 'Build API'). This overhaul makes the Objectives module a first-class, LLM-assisted planning tool.

key_results.metric: KR format supports descriptive text — backend schema changed to accept description field alongside or instead of metric+baseline+target
LLM enrichment available during objective creation — user can invoke Skills (deep-explore, etc.) from create/edit objective form
Guidelines individually assignable to objectives — UI supports attaching specific G-NNN IDs beyond scope-based matching
Knowledge objects linkable to objectives — knowledge_ids field has working UI with search/select
Optional dependencies between objectives — depends_on field with UI visualization showing O→O relationships

assumptions: Changing KR schema from numeric-only to descriptive is backward-compatible — existing numeric KRs continue to work
LLM enrichment uses existing Skills infrastructure (deep-explore, deep-verify) and existing LLM provider adapters
Individual guideline assignment coexists with scope-based assignment — both mechanisms work simultaneously
Objective dependencies are optional and do not block pipeline task execution (advisory, not enforced)

objectives.title: Skills Management — globalny system tworzenia, zarzadzania i udostepniania Skills
objectives.description:
Zbudowac kompletny system zarzadzania Skills jako GLOBALNY zasob platformy — niezalezny od projektow. Projekty korzystaja ze Skills, ale Skills istnieja na poziomie platformy (jak szablony). Kluczowe zalozenia:

1. **Skills sa globalne** — nie naleza do zadnego projektu. Kazdy projekt moze uzywac dowolnych ACTIVE skills. Analogia: Skills to 'app store' narzedzi, projekty to 'uzytkownicy'.

2. **Tworzenie zgodne z Anthropic skill-creator** (https://github.com/anthropics/skills/tree/main/skills/skill-creator) — kazdy skill MUSI miec SKILL.md z YAML frontmatter (name, description), opcjonalne zasoby (scripts/, references/, assets/), i testy (evals/evals.json). Framework zapewnia: iteracyjne ulepszanie, testowanie z graderem, benchmarking.

3. **Pelne CRUD w UI** — dodawanie (kreator z edytorem SKILL.md), przegladanie (lista/grid z filtrami), edycja (inline editor), usuwanie (z walidacja zaleznosci — ostrzezenie jesli skill jest uzywany przez aktywne taski).

4. **Lifecycle statusow** — DRAFT (w budowie, niewidoczny w task assignment) → ACTIVE (promowany, dostepny do uzycia) → DEPRECATED (ostrzezenie przy nowym przypisaniu) → ARCHIVED (ukryty). Promocja DRAFT→ACTIVE wymaga: valid SKILL.md + min. 1 eval test case + TESLint pass (0 errors).

5. **Kategoryzacja i grupowanie** — category (workflow, analysis, generation, validation, integration, refactoring, testing, deployment, documentation, custom), tagi, scopes. UI: browsing po kategorii, filtrowanie po statusie/tagach, wyszukiwanie, przelaczanie widoku grupowania.

6. **Backend** — nowy endpoint globalny (NIE pod /projects/{slug}/): GET/POST /api/v1/skills, GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/skills/{id}. Osobna tabela 'skills' w PostgreSQL bez klucza obcego do projects. Taski referencja skill_id.

7. **Frontend** — 'Skills' jako top-level pozycja w glownym menu, na rowni z Projects, Dashboard.

8. **Weryfikacja TESLint** (https://github.com/Deep-Process/TESLint) — statyczny linter dla konfiguracji Claude Code (w tym SKILL.md). 35 regul w 7 kategoriach: Structure (S), References (R), Consistency (C), Tokens (T), Completeness (X), Best Practices (B), Security (P). Deterministyczny, offline, bez LLM calls. Uzywany jako: (a) gate przy promocji DRAFT→ACTIVE (0 errors wymagane), (b) on-demand linting w edytorze SKILL.md (inline warnings), (c) CI/CD check w pipeline. Konfigurowalne przez .teslintrc.json (wylaczanie regul, progi, severity).

key_results.metric: Backend: globalne endpointy API dla Skills (CRUD + filtrowanie + search)
key_results.description: GET /api/v1/skills (list+filter), POST /api/v1/skills (create), GET /api/v1/skills/{id} (detail), PATCH /api/v1/skills/{id} (update), DELETE /api/v1/skills/{id} (delete), POST /api/v1/skills/{id}/promote (status transition)

key_results.metric: PostgreSQL schemat + migracja dla tabeli skills (globalna, bez project_id FK)
key_results.description: Tabela skills: id, ext_id (S-NNN), name, description, category, status (DRAFT/ACTIVE/DEPRECATED/ARCHIVED), skill_md_content, tags[], scopes[], evals_json, metadata, created_at, updated_at. GIN index na tags i scopes. Bez klucza obcego do projects — skills sa globalne.

key_results.metric: Frontend: strona Skills w glownym menu z pelnym CRUD
key_results.description: Top-level nav item 'Skills'. Widoki: lista/grid z filtrami (category, status, tags, search), formularz tworzenia (kreator z SKILL.md editor, kategoria, tagi), widok szczegolowy (SKILL.md preview, metadata, usage stats, evals), edycja inline, usuwanie z potwierdzeniem.

key_results.metric: System promocji DRAFT→ACTIVE z walidacja (valid SKILL.md + evals + TESLint pass)
key_results.description: Przycisk 'Promote' widoczny tylko na DRAFT skills. Walidacja przed promocja (3 gates): (1) SKILL.md ma poprawny frontmatter YAML (name, description), (2) minimum 1 eval test case w evals, (3) TESLint pass — 0 errors (warnings dozwolone). Wynik TESLint wyswietlany w UI przed promocja — uzytkownik widzi liste findings z severity. Jesli TESLint wykryje errors: domyslnie blokada promocji, ALE uzytkownik moze wykonac force override — przycisk 'Promote anyway' (wyrozniajacy sie kolorem ostrzegawczym, np. czerwony/pomaranczowy) z wymaganym potwierdzeniem ('Ten skill ma X errors — czy na pewno chcesz promowac?'). Force-promoted skills dostaja flage 'promoted_with_warnings: true' i badge w UI (np. zolty trojkat ostrzegawczy obok zielonego ACTIVE). Historia promocji zapisuje: kto promowal, kiedy, ile errors/warnings bylo w momencie promocji, czy uzyto force override. UI rozroznia DRAFT (badge pomaranczowy) vs ACTIVE (badge zielony) vs ACTIVE+warnings (badge zielony + trojkat) vs DEPRECATED (badge szary).

key_results.metric: Kategoryzacja i grupowanie — filtry, widoki, wyszukiwanie w UI
key_results.description: Sidebar lub tab-bar z kategoriami (workflow, analysis, generation, validation, ...). Filtrowanie po: category, status, tags, scopes. Wyszukiwanie po nazwie/opisie. Toggle widoku: grupowanie po kategorii / po statusie / po scope / flat list.

key_results.metric: Integracja z taskami — task moze referencjowac globalny skill_id
key_results.description: Task.skill_id wskazuje na globalny skill (nie sciezke do pliku). W formularzu tworzenia taska — dropdown z ACTIVE skills (filtrowany po scope match). W widoku szczegolowym skill — lista taskow ktore go uzywaja (usage tracking).

key_results.metric: TESLint integracja — statyczny linting SKILL.md w edytorze i jako promotion gate
key_results.description: Integracja TESLint (https://github.com/Deep-Process/TESLint) na 3 poziomach: (1) Inline w edytorze SKILL.md — uruchomienie lintingu on-demand, wyswietlenie findings jako inline annotations (error/warning/info) z opisem reguly i sugestia naprawy. (2) Promotion gate — przed DRAFT→ACTIVE automatyczne uruchomienie TESLint, blokada jesli errors > 0 (z mozliwoscia force override przez uzytkownika), wyswietlenie raportu z podsumowaniem (X errors, Y warnings po kategoriach S/R/C/T/X/B/P). (3) Backend endpoint POST /api/v1/skills/{id}/lint — uruchamia TESLint na SKILL.md content, zwraca JSON z findings. Endpoint POST /api/v1/skills/{id}/promote przyjmuje opcjonalny parametr force: true do pominiecia blokady TESLint errors. Konfiguracja lintingu per-skill mozliwa przez .teslintrc.json przechowywany w metadata.

assumptions: Skills sa globalnym zasobem platformy — NIE naleza do projektow (brak project_id FK w tabeli skills)
Kazdy projekt moze uzywac dowolnych ACTIVE skills — referencja przez skill_id w task
Format skill zgodny z Anthropic skill-creator: SKILL.md + opcjonalne scripts/references/assets + evals/evals.json
Promocja DRAFT→ACTIVE to explicite dzialanie uzytkownika z walidacja — nie automatyczne
Usuwanie ACTIVE skill wymaga potwierdzenia i sprawdzenia czy nie jest uzywany przez IN_PROGRESS taski
Endpointy Skills sa globalne (/api/v1/skills), nie pod /projects/{slug}/
SKILL.md content przechowywany jako TEXT w PostgreSQL — edycja przez textarea z syntax highlighting
Evals przechowywane jako JSONB w PostgreSQL — edycja przez structured form
TESLint (https://github.com/Deep-Process/TESLint) zainstalowany jako dependency backendu — Python package z PyYAML
TESLint uruchamiany server-side (nie w przegladarce) — backend tworzy temp dir ze SKILL.md i uruchamia python -m teslint
TESLint konfiguracja (.teslintrc.json) opcjonalna per-skill — domyslne reguly wystarczaja dla wiekszosci przypadkow
TESLint exit code 0 = pass, 1 = errors — errors domyslnie blokuja promocje (warnings informacyjne), ale uzytkownik moze force override z potwierdzeniem

objectives.title: LLM Core Integration — platforma AI z tool-use, uprawnieniami i uniwersalnym chatem
objectives.description: Zbudowac corowa infrastrukture LLM jako FUNDAMENTALNA funkcjonalnosc ForgePrime — nie jednorazowa integracja, a PLATFORMA do interakcji z AI w calym systemie. Stan obecny: adaptery providerow istnieja w core/llm/ (Anthropic, OpenAI), ale forge-api/app/routers/ai.py zwraca MOCK dane — zaden prawdziwy LLM call nie jest wykonywany. Cel: (1) Uruchomic ZYWE polaczenie z LLM — provider management, API key storage, health check, model selection. (2) Tool Registry — zdefiniowac i zarejestrowac narzedzia Forge API ktore LLM moze wywolywac (CRUD encji, search, lint, status changes). To jak CLI gdzie wpisujesz polecenia i LLM wykonuje uzywajac narzedzi. (3) Permission System — konfigurowalny dostep LLM do zasobow: per-projekt (np. objectives: read-only, tasks: read-write) i globalnie (np. skills: read-write, projects: create). Admin moze ograniczyc co LLM widzi i modyfikuje. (4) Reusable Chat Component — uniwersalny komponent React embedowalny w dowolnym module (skills, objectives, ideas, tasks, knowledge...). Renderuje konwersacje, streaming odpowiedzi, wyswietla tool calls LLM (co robi), historia wiadomosci. (5) Context System — LLM automatycznie otrzymuje focus context: jesli uzytkownik edytuje skill S-003, LLM wie ze pracujemy nad tym skillem i ma jego dane. Jesli uzytkownik jest na stronie objectives, LLM wie o aktywnych celach. (6) Feature Flags — per-modul wlaczanie/wylaczanie LLM: {'skills': true, 'objectives': true, 'tasks': false, ...}. Ustawienia w UI. (7) Session Management — kazda konwersacja to sesja z historia, tokenami, kosztami. Sesje widoczne w Debug Console (O-007). (8) Contract System — formalne kontrakty dla roznych typow interakcji LLM (skill-edit, entity-suggest, plan-decompose). Kontrakty definiuja: input schema, output schema, available tools, system prompt template. Rejestr kontraktow w konfiguracji.

key_results.metric: Live LLM provider connection — prawdziwe wywolania Claude API dzialajace end-to-end (prompt → response → display)
key_results.description: Backend: aktywacja adapterow z core/llm/providers/ w forge-api. Endpoint /api/v1/llm/providers (list configured), /api/v1/llm/providers/{id}/test (health check), /api/v1/llm/chat (send message, get response). Frontend: UI do konfiguracji providerow (API keys, default model, test connection). Start z Claude (Anthropic), architektura provider-agnostic (OpenAI, Ollama ready).

key_results.metric: Tool Registry — narzedzia Forge API dostepne dla LLM z definicjami JSON Schema
key_results.description: Minimum 15 narzedzi: getSkill, updateSkill, listSkills, createSkill, deleteSkill, getEntity (generic), updateEntity, listEntities, searchEntities, runLint, getProject, getObjectives, getTasks, getKnowledge, getGuidelines. Kazde narzedzie ma: name, description, parameters (JSON Schema), required permissions. Tool registry jako konfiguracja — mozliwosc dodawania custom tools.

key_results.metric: Permission System — konfigurowalny dostep LLM do zasobow (per-projekt i globalnie)
key_results.description: Model uprawnien: {module: {read: bool, write: bool, delete: bool}}. Dwa poziomy: (1) Global permissions — co LLM moze robic na poziomie platformy (tworzyc projekty, edytowac skills, etc.), (2) Project permissions — co LLM moze robic w konkretnym projekcie (read objectives, write tasks, etc.). UI w Settings do konfiguracji. Backend middleware sprawdza uprawnienia przed wykonaniem tool call. Default: conservative (read-only), user moze rozszerzac.

key_results.metric: Reusable LLM Chat Component — komponent React embedowalny w dowolnym module
key_results.description: Komponent <LLMChat context={...} tools={...} permissions={...} />. Funkcjonalnosci: (1) input textarea z send button + Enter to send, (2) message history (user/assistant/tool-call), (3) streaming response display, (4) tool call visualization (nazwa narzedzia, parametry, wynik — expandable), (5) context indicator (co LLM widzi), (6) token counter + cost estimate, (7) session persistence (reload nie traci historii), (8) resize/minimize/maximize. Komponent uzywa WebSocket do streaming. Styl: ciemny panel boczny lub dolny (konfigurowalny).

key_results.metric: Context System — LLM automatycznie otrzymuje focus context z aktualnego widoku
key_results.description: Kazdy modul dostarcza context provider: {module: 'skills', entity_type: 'skill', entity_id: 'S-003', data: {...full skill data...}, related: [{type: 'project', slug: 'my-project'}]}. LLM system prompt zawiera: 'Uzytkownik pracuje nad [entity_type] [entity_id]. Oto dane: [data].' Context odswiezany przy nawigacji. Uzytkownik moze rozszerzac kontekst ('dodaj tez objectives z projektu X').

key_results.metric: Feature Flags — per-modul wlaczanie/wylaczanie LLM chat z UI Settings
key_results.description: Konfiguracja w /settings: toggle per-modul (skills, objectives, ideas, tasks, knowledge, guidelines, decisions, lessons, dashboard). Zapisywana w backend (global config). Gdy LLM wylaczony dla modulu — chat component nie renderuje sie. Default: skills=true, reszta=false (stopniowe wlaczanie).

key_results.metric: LLM Session Management — historia konwersacji, tokeny, koszty, powiazanie z Debug Console
key_results.description: Kazda konwersacja = session z: session_id, messages[], total_tokens (in/out), estimated_cost, model_used, context_snapshot, tool_calls_log. Sesje przechowywane w Redis (TTL 24h) lub PostgreSQL. Widoczne w Debug Console (O-007) jako LLM Monitor entries. API: GET /api/v1/llm/sessions, GET /api/v1/llm/sessions/{id}.

key_results.metric: Provider Management UI — konfiguracja providerow LLM w interfejsie (API keys, modele, test)
key_results.description: Strona /settings/llm-providers z: (1) lista skonfigurowanych providerow (Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama), (2) formularz dodawania: nazwa, typ, API key (masked), base URL (dla Ollama), default model, (3) test connection button (sends simple prompt, shows response), (4) model selection per-provider, (5) cost tracking dashboard (total tokens today/week/month). API keys przechowywane securely (encrypted in DB, never exposed in API responses).

assumptions: Adaptery LLM w core/llm/providers/ (Anthropic, OpenAI) sa funkcjonalne — wymagaja tylko aktywacji w forge-api
Claude (Anthropic) jako pierwszy provider — wystarczy ANTHROPIC_API_KEY w env
LiteLLM NIE jest wymagany na poczatku — bezposrednie adaptery wystarczaja, LiteLLM mozna dodac pozniej jako transport layer
Tool-use (function calling) jest kluczowe — LLM musi moc wywolywac narzedzia, nie tylko generowac tekst
Permission system jest per-conversation scope, nie per-request (raz ustalone dla sesji, nie sprawdzane per tool call — performance)
WebSocket jest juz gotowy (ws.ts + ws.py) — potrzebny nowy event type: llm.chunk, llm.tool_call, llm.complete
Chat component MUSI byc reusable — ten sam komponent w Skills, Objectives, Tasks, etc. z roznym context
Session history w Redis (fast) z opcjonalnym archiwum w PostgreSQL (persistent)
API keys NIGDY nie sa eksponowane w API responses — stored encrypted, transmitted only server-side to provider
Debug Console (O-007) konsumuje session data — O-010 emituje eventy, O-007 je wyswietla
Feature flags sa runtime-configurable — nie wymagaja restartu serwera
Contract system rozszerza istniejacy core/llm/contract.py — dodaje tool definitions i permission scoping

objectives.title: Skills LLM Assistant — AI chat w edytorze Skills z multi-file support i agentskills.io compliance
objectives.description: Pierwsza pelna implementacja LLM Core (O-010) w edytorze Skills. Cel: uzytkownik otwiera /skills/new lub /skills/{id}, widzi split view — po lewej reczna edycja SKILL.md i plikow. AI asystent dostepny przez globalny Sidebar AI (O-014) — NIE potrzeba dedykowanego czatu w edytorze. LLM widzi aktualny skill, moze go modyfikowac przez Forge API tools, moze czytac inne skills na zadanie uzytkownika. Rozszerzenie modelu skills o multi-file support (SKILL.md + scripts/ + references/ + assets/) zgodnie z agentskills.io specification. Drag & drop plikow ktore LLM odczyta i wykorzysta do tworzenia/modyfikacji skilla. LLM operuje TYLKO przez Forge API — nie modyfikuje plikow bezposrednio, wywoluje updateSkill(), addSkillFile(), runLint(), modifyMetadata(). TESLint validation zintegrowany z workflow LLM — LLM moze uruchomic lint i interpretowac wyniki. Skill creator workflow inspirowany Anthropic skill-creator: intent capture → research → write → test → iterate.

key_results.metric: Split view w edytorze Skills — lewa strona: edytor plikow, prawa strona: metadata/evals/lint tabs (BEZ dedykowanego chat panelu — Sidebar AI wystarczy)
key_results.description: Przebudowa SkillEditor.tsx: file tree (SKILL.md, scripts/, references/, assets/) + editor aktywnego pliku + metadata tabs. BEZ dedykowanego panelu czatu — globalny Sidebar AI (O-014) zapewnia kontekstowa pomoc AI na stronie skills. Usunac istniejace przyciski Generate with AI i AI Chat z edytora.

key_results.metric: Multi-file skills — backend i frontend obsluguja SKILL.md + scripts/ + references/ + assets/ + custom pliki
key_results.description: Rozszerzenie modelu Skill o pole 'files': [{path: 'scripts/validate.py', content: '...', type: 'script'}, {path: 'references/API.md', content: '...', type: 'reference'}, ...]. Backend: PATCH /api/v1/skills/{id}/files (add/update/delete files). Frontend: file tree component z: add file, rename, delete, drag reorder. Zgodne z agentskills.io spec: SKILL.md (required), scripts/ (executable code), references/ (documentation), assets/ (templates, resources). Mozliwosc dodawania custom directories.

key_results.metric: Skill-specific LLM tools — narzedzia dostepne dla LLM w kontekscie edycji Skills
key_results.description: Minimum 8 skill-specific tools: (1) updateSkillContent — zmien SKILL.md content, (2) addSkillFile — dodaj plik do scripts/references/assets, (3) removeSkillFile — usun plik, (4) updateSkillMetadata — zmien category/tags/scopes, (5) runSkillLint — uruchom TESLint i zwroc wyniki, (6) getOtherSkill — pobierz tresc innego skilla (na zadanie usera: 'skopiuj z S-005'), (7) instantiateACTemplate — uzyj szablonu AC, (8) previewSkill — pokaz preview SKILL.md po zmianach. Kazde narzedzie scopowane do biezacego skilla (nie moze modyfikowac innych bez explicit permission).

key_results.metric: Drag & drop plikow — przesylanie plikow do kontekstu LLM (PDF, MD, TXT, code files)
key_results.description: Drop zone w chat panelu: (1) uzytkownik przeciaga plik z komputera, (2) plik uploadowany do backend (temp storage), (3) zawartosc pliku dodana do kontekstu LLM ('Uzytkownik przeslal plik {name}: {content}'), (4) LLM moze uzyc zawartosci do stworzenia/modyfikacji skilla. Obsluywane formaty: .md, .txt, .py, .js, .ts, .json, .yaml, .pdf (text extraction). Limit: 10 plikow per sesja, 1MB per plik. Uploadowane pliki widoczne w chat jako attachments.

key_results.metric: agentskills.io spec compliance — walidacja struktury skill zgodnie ze specyfikacja
key_results.description: Walidacja: (1) name: 1-64 chars, lowercase + hyphens, no consecutive hyphens, no leading/trailing hyphen, (2) description: 1-1024 chars, non-empty, (3) SKILL.md < 500 lines recommended (warning, not error), (4) frontmatter: valid YAML z required fields (name, description), (5) optional fields validated: license, compatibility (max 500 chars), metadata (string→string), allowed-tools (space-delimited). Walidacja uruchamiana: on save, on promote, on LLM updateSkillContent. Wyniki widoczne inline w edytorze.

key_results.metric: AI-assisted skill creation — via global Sidebar AI (nie dedykowany chat w edytorze)
key_results.description: Sidebar AI (O-014) automatycznie wykrywa scope skills i oferuje kontekstowa pomoc. Uzytkownik moze iterowac nad SKILL.md w edytorze korzystajac z globalnego Sidebar AI. Usunac przycisk Generate with AI z SkillEditor — Sidebar AI zastepuje te funkcjonalnosc.

assumptions: O-010 (LLM Core) jest WYMAGANY przed ta praca — chat component, tool registry, permissions musza istniec
Istniejacy SkillEditor.tsx (670 linii) bedzie przebudowany — nie nowy komponent od zera, refactor z zachowaniem istniejacych funkcji
Multi-file storage w backend: opcja A (JSONB array w skill row) lub opcja B (osobna tabela skill_files) — do decyzji architektonicznej
File upload: temp storage w /tmp lub Redis (TTL 1h), NIE persistent — pliki to context dla LLM, nie staly zasob
agentskills.io validation moze zastapic lub uzupelnic TESLint — dwie warstwy walidacji (spec compliance + best practices)
LLM tools sa scopowane: w kontekscie skills edytora, LLM ma dostep do skill-specific tools + generic read tools (getEntity, search)
Anthropic skill-creator workflow to INSPIRACJA, nie kopia — adaptujemy do naszego UI/UX
PDF text extraction: prosty parser server-side (pdfplumber lub pdf2text), nie OCR
SkillEditor zachowuje obecny layout (editor + metadata tabs). Dedykowany panel czatu USUNIETY — Sidebar AI (O-014) zastepuje go. Przyciski Generate with AI i AI Chat do usuniecia.

objectives.title: Layout Overhaul — 3-column architecture with top navigation bar
objectives.description: Transform the current 2-column layout (left sidebar + main) into a 3-column layout (left context panel + main workspace + right AI sidebar slot) with a horizontal top navigation bar. This is the structural foundation for the AI Sidebar (O-014) and all subsequent UI improvements. The current global Sidebar.tsx becomes a TopNavBar. The ProjectSidebar moves into a left context panel that changes per view. A resizable right sidebar slot is created for the AI Sidebar. DEEP-VERIFY FINDINGS: (1) 4 files use calc(100vh-X) that will break with top nav — must update to use CSS variable --topnav-height, (2) project layout -m-6 hack must be eliminated by removing p-6 from root main, (3) ProjectSidebar width is 224px (w-56) not 280px — reconcile, (4) BottomPanel fullscreen calc needs updating, (5) right sidebar should default to COLLAPSED since O-014 populates it later.

key_results.metric: Top navigation bar renders correctly on all route types
key_results.description: Horizontal top nav with: Forge logo, Dashboard, Projects, Skills (left-aligned), Settings (right-aligned). Active section highlighted. Replaces current vertical global Sidebar. Route types: (1) / Dashboard, (2) /projects list, (3) /projects/[slug]/* project detail, (4) /skills list, (5) /skills/[id] detail, (6) /skills/new create, (7) /settings/llm settings. Note: Settings href is /settings/llm not /settings. 'User' nav item deferred until auth system exists.

key_results.metric: Left context panel renders view-specific content
key_results.description: Left panel (collapsible) shows different content per view: (1) Skills page: Section label + Categories filter list (currently w-48/192px aside in skills/page.tsx), (2) Project pages: ProjectSidebar (existing, moved here — currently w-56/224px), (3) Dashboard: quick navigation or empty. Uses React context/provider pattern — each page calls useLeftPanel(content) to declare its left panel content. Width adapts to content (not forced to 280px — uses ProjectSidebar's existing 224px or Skills' 192px). See D-034.

key_results.metric: Right sidebar slot exists with collapse/expand and drag-to-resize
key_results.description: Right column (default 420px, min 320px, max 600px) with: drag handle for resize, collapse/expand button, width and collapsed state persisted to localStorage. DEFAULTS TO COLLAPSED — populated later by O-014 (AI Sidebar). Smooth CSS transition on collapse. When collapsed: thin strip (~40px) with expand button. See D-038 for minimum content area width.

key_results.metric: No visual regressions on existing pages
key_results.description: All existing pages render correctly in the new layout. KNOWN FILES REQUIRING calc() UPDATES: (1) skills/new/page.tsx (100vh-4rem), (2) skills/[id]/page.tsx (100vh-4rem), (3) projects/[slug]/execution/[taskId]/page.tsx (100vh-12rem), (4) components/debug/BottomPanel.tsx (100vh-3rem). All must use CSS var --topnav-height. The -m-6 hack in projects/[slug]/layout.tsx must be eliminated by removing p-6 from root main. ProjectSidebar mobile drawer logic needs refactoring for new layout container.

assumptions: Global Sidebar.tsx (4 nav items: Dashboard, Projects, Skills, Settings) transforms into horizontal TopNavBar
ProjectSidebar.tsx moves into left context panel — mobile drawer logic needs refactoring, width stays at w-56 (224px)
Skills page categories aside (w-48, currently inline in page.tsx with hidden lg:block) extracts into left context panel
Right sidebar slot defaults to COLLAPSED — empty container until O-014 populates it
CSS Grid for 3-column body (grid-template-columns: auto 1fr auto), Flexbox for top nav
Mobile/responsive design can be deferred — add hidden md:block on left/right columns for basic safety
New CSS variables: --topnav-height, --left-panel-width, --right-sidebar-width — all calc(100vh-X) patterns must reference these
Remove p-6 from root layout main — let each page manage its own padding. Eliminates the -m-6 hack in project layout
BottomPanel spans full width below all 3 columns. Its fullscreen calc must use --topnav-height
CommandPalette (modal overlay) unaffected by layout restructuring

objectives.title: Scope System and Capability Contract Engine — context-aware AI permissions
objectives.description: Build the scope resolution system that maps routes to scopes, and the capability contract engine that defines what operations the AI can perform per scope. This is the 'brain' of the AI Sidebar. DEEP-VERIFY FINDINGS: (1) Only skills scope has dedicated backend tools (4 tools) — other scopes rely on 4 generic read tools with ZERO write/delete tools. Capability contracts for non-skills scopes must mark write/delete operations as 'available: false' / 'Coming soon'. (2) Scopes (multi-valued, route-derived: 'skills') are distinct from context_type (single-valued, entity-based: 'skill') — need a mapping layer. (3) No per-user permissions exist — use global LLM config permissions. See D-035, D-036, D-037.

key_results.metric: Route-to-scope auto-detection for all page routes
key_results.description: useScopeResolver() hook maps pathname to scopes. Core mappings: / -> ['dashboard'], /skills -> ['skills'], /skills/[id] -> ['skills'], /skills/new -> ['skills'], /projects -> ['projects'], /projects/[slug] -> ['projects', slug], /projects/[slug]/tasks -> ['tasks'], /projects/[slug]/tasks/[id] -> ['tasks'], /projects/[slug]/objectives -> ['objectives'], /projects/[slug]/objectives/[id] -> ['objectives'], /projects/[slug]/ideas -> ['ideas'], /projects/[slug]/decisions -> ['decisions'], /projects/[slug]/knowledge -> ['knowledge'], /projects/[slug]/guidelines -> ['guidelines'], /projects/[slug]/changes -> ['changes'], /projects/[slug]/lessons -> ['lessons'], /projects/[slug]/ac-templates -> ['ac_templates'], /projects/[slug]/board -> ['tasks'], /projects/[slug]/execution/[taskId] -> ['tasks'], /settings/llm -> ['settings']. Detail pages inherit parent scope. Must also define scope-to-context_type mapping (see D-035): scope 'skills' maps to context_type 'skill', scope 'tasks' maps to context_type 'task', etc.

key_results.metric: Capability contracts defined per scope with operations and action types
key_results.description: Static capability registry defining per-scope operations. 6 scopes: skills, tasks, objectives, ideas, decisions, knowledge. Each operation has: id, label, description, action type (READ/WRITE/DELETE), mapped tool name, and availability flag. IMPORTANT (see D-036): Only skills scope has full tool coverage (listEntities, searchEntities, getEntity, getOtherSkill, updateSkillContent, updateSkillMetadata, runSkillLint). Other scopes have READ tools only (listEntities, searchEntities, getEntity — generic). Write/delete operations for tasks, objectives, ideas, decisions, knowledge marked as available: false with UI showing 'Coming soon' until dedicated tools are created.

key_results.metric: Capabilities respect global LLM permissions — disabled operations show 'No permission'
key_results.description: Capability contract UI cross-references GLOBAL LLM permissions from GET /api/v1/llm/config (not per-user — see D-037). PermissionEngine has 10 modules with read/write/delete each. If LLM config has write=false for skills, the 'Create skill' capability shows 'No permission' and toggle is disabled. Operations with available: false (no backend tool) show 'Coming soon'. Backend enforces via tool filtering in get_tools(). Additionally, add execution-level permission check in ToolRegistry.execute() as defense-in-depth.

key_results.metric: User can toggle capabilities on/off per conversation session
key_results.description: Toggle state stored in sidebarStore (Zustand, created by O-014). Defaults restored when user creates a new conversation. User can also save persistent defaults in Settings page. Toggles are per-conversation, not global. When user toggles off 'Create skill', the frontend sends it in disabled_capabilities on next chat request. Only toggleable for operations where available=true AND permission=granted.

key_results.metric: Backend chat endpoint accepts scopes and disabled capabilities, constrains tools
key_results.description: POST /api/v1/llm/chat extended with: scopes: string[] (active scopes), disabled_capabilities: string[] (tool names to exclude). Scopes are mapped to context_types (see D-035) — e.g., scopes=['skills','tasks'] maps to context_types=['skill','task']. ToolRegistry.get_tools() extended to accept context_types: list[str] (plural — union of tools from all types) and disabled_tools: list[str] (tool names to exclude). AgentLoop receives only allowed tools.

assumptions: Scopes map to existing PermissionEngine modules — no new permission model needed
ToolRegistry.get_tools() extended to accept context_types: list[str] (plural) returning union of tools from all matching types, plus disabled_tools: list[str] to exclude
Capability contracts are static frontend definitions — no separate database table
Permissions come from global LLM config (GET /api/v1/llm/config), NOT per-user auth (no per-user permissions exist yet)
Scope-to-context_type naming mapping needed: scope 'skills' -> context_type 'skill' (plural to singular), defined in shared constants
Operations without backend tools marked available: false — UI shows 'Coming soon', not 'No permission'
Scope changes are real-time — no page reload needed when user adds/removes scopes in sidebar
Backend work (tool filtering) independent of frontend layout changes — can proceed in parallel with O-012
Add execution-level permission check in ToolRegistry.execute() as defense-in-depth (currently only filters at tool-list level)

objectives.title: AI Sidebar Shell — resizable chat panel with tools, scopes, and conversations
objectives.description: Build the right-side AI Sidebar component that populates the layout slot from O-012. DEEP-VERIFY FINDINGS: (1) LLMChat has its own header that would duplicate sidebar header — need hideHeader/embedded prop, (2) chatStore lacks loadSessions() and resumeSession() methods needed for conversations list — must be added here, (3) scope 'skills' (plural) vs context_type 'skill' (singular) naming mismatch needs mapping from O-013, (4) sidebarStore must use Zustand persist middleware for localStorage, (5) current chatStore.sendMessage() blocks on HTTP response showing 'Thinking...' — not true token-by-token streaming — WS event wiring may need fixing.

key_results.metric: AI Sidebar renders on all pages with scope auto-detection
Tools tab and Scopes tab with correct content
Conversations list with create, resume, delete
Sidebar collapsible and resizable with persistent state
Page-level visibility config via hiddenRoutes

assumptions: O-012 provides the right sidebar slot (defaults to collapsed) — this objective populates it
O-013 provides scope resolution, capability contracts, and scope-to-context_type mapping — this objective displays them
LLMChat.tsx needs hideHeader/embedded prop — sidebar shell provides its own header (scope chips, tabs), LLMChat's built-in header (AI Chat + context badge + token counter) would be redundant
chatStore.ts must be extended with: loadSessions(), resumeSession(), sessionList state — these are PREREQUISITES for the conversations list
LLMChat, Message, ChatInput, ToolCallBlock can render at 420px width — verified: no hardcoded min-width, Message uses max-w-[85%], code blocks have overflow-x-auto
Skills data for Tools tab comes from existing skillStore — no new API needed
Session list from GET /api/v1/llm/sessions — filtered client-side by context_type using scope-to-context_type mapping
sidebarStore.ts (new Zustand store with persist middleware) manages: width, collapsed, activeTab, pageVisibility, activeScopes, disabledCapabilities
Sidebar chat and skill editor chat (O-011) coexist as separate instances with different session namespaces (sidebar: 'sidebar-{scope}', skill editor: 'skill-{id}')
WS streaming: chatStore.handleWsEvent() handlers exist for chat.token/tool_call/complete but may not be wired to actual WS subscription — verify and fix if needed

objectives.title: LLM Deep Context & Chat UX — scope negotiation, entity context, slash commands, streaming
objectives.description: Upgrade LLM integration so AI understands what the user sees and edits, can negotiate missing scopes, receives entity content (not just page summary), and has polished chat UX (slash commands, WS streaming, conversation search). This is the bridge between O-017 (page annotations) and O-019 (multi-session orchestration).

key_results.metric: LLM receives full entity content when user views/edits an entity
Scope negotiation: LLM detects missing scope and proposes adding it
Slash-command autocomplete in chat input
Real-time WS streaming with tool call display
LLM system prompt includes project context, guidelines, scopes, and page context
Cross-scope conversation search

assumptions: O-014 provides the AI Sidebar shell with conversations list and chatStore.loadSessions/resumeSession — this objective adds behavior on top
O-013 provides capability contracts and backend scopes/disabled_capabilities support — this objective makes the LLM aware of them via system prompt
ChatInput.tsx extended with autocomplete — substantial work (~150-200 lines), consider SlashCommandDropdown as sub-component to keep ChatInput manageable
Slash command trigger: only when '/' is at position 0 in input (avoids false triggers mid-sentence)
SessionManager search: simple Python-side str.find() on Redis-stored messages, limited to last 50 sessions. Not scalable but adequate for 24h TTL session volume
Cross-scope search is an optional enhancement — core chat works without it
Streaming fix: current sendMessage() awaits HTTP POST response and replaces placeholder. True streaming requires WS event wiring (chat.token events) + refactored display flow
Route order in llm_chat.py: GET /sessions/search BEFORE GET /sessions/{session_id} to avoid FastAPI path parameter conflict
ContextPayload.to_system_prompt() is the correct extension point (NOT ContextResolver — the method lives on the dataclass)

objectives.title: File-Based Skills Storage with Git Sync
objectives.description: Refaktoryzacja warstwy storage skills z monolitycznego JSON-blob (_global/skills.json) na plikowy model katalogowy z git-backed repo (ForgePrime/forge-skills.git). Każdy skill jako katalog z _config.json + SKILL.md + pliki. Git sync umożliwia wersjonowanie, dystrybucję i współdzielenie skills. UI wspiera upload, drag&drop między folderami, i zarządzanie plikami.

key_results.description: Backend: nowy SkillStorageService operujący na katalogach (_global/skills/{name}/) zamiast monolitycznego JSON. API operuje po nazwie skilla (slug), nazwa unikalna. Indeks w DB (name, categories, tags, status, path) aktualizowany przy create/edit/sync — pliki = source of truth, DB = cache do szybkiego wyszukiwania/filtrowania.
key_results.current: DONE: SkillStorageService with per-directory storage, _index.json cache, name-based API routing

key_results.description: Każdy skill ma wymagany SKILL.md (auto-generowany przy create, source of truth dla name/description) + _config.json (categories[], tags[], status, evals, promotion_history) + pliki (scripts/, references/, assets/). Walidacja spójności name między frontmatterem a UI (bidirectional sync).
key_results.current: DONE: SKILL.md auto-generated, _config.json with categories/tags/status/evals, frontmatter parsing

key_results.description: Git sync backend + UI: endpointy API pull/push/status + GitSyncService. W UI: sync to repo (commit scoped + push), na stronie /skills: scan repo po nowe skills i wczytanie, push lokalnych zmian, status synchronizacji. Flaga sync per skill w _config.json.
key_results.current: DONE: GitSyncService with pull/push/status/init, UI sync bar on /skills, Sync to Repo button in editor, sync toggle per skill

key_results.description: Category model zmieniony na wielokrotny (categories: string[] zamiast category: string) — zmiana API, typów frontend, formularzy, filtrowania.
key_results.current: DONE: categories[] multi-select in editor, multi-category filtering in list, category badges on SkillRow

key_results.description: File management w edytorze: upload plików do file tree sidebar (drag&drop + przycisk), przenoszenie między katalogami (scripts/references/assets), pytanie o nadpisanie gdy plik istnieje, usuwanie z panelu narzędzi po prawej z confirm dialog. Upload nowych skills jako folder/zip.
key_results.current: DONE: drag-drop upload, per-folder upload, drag-to-move between folders, overwrite detection, delete with confirmation

key_results.description: Ujednolicony UI: tworzenie nowego skilla otwiera ten sam pełny SkillEditor co edycja (file tree, metadata, lint, promote). Przeprojektowany interfejs Skills z lepszym UX.
key_results.current: DONE: unified SkillEditor for create/edit, full editor with file tree + metadata + lint + promote on create

key_results.description: Migracja istniejących skills z skills.json do nowego formatu katalogowego (follow-up, po nowym storage).
key_results.current: DONE: migrate_skills.py script with backup, dry-run, category->categories[], index rebuild

assumptions: ForgePrime/forge-skills.git repo istnieje i jest dostępne
Git jest zainstalowany na maszynie hostującej Forge API (git sync opcjonalny — bez git działa local-only)
API zmienia się kontrolowanie: nowy routing po nazwie, categories[] zamiast category. Frontend adaptuje się w ramach tego objective.
Frontmatter SKILL.md jest source of truth dla name/description/version/allowed-tools, z bidirectional sync do UI
_config.json jest source of truth dla categories, tags, status, evals, promotion_history
SKILL.md jest wymagany — auto-generowany przy tworzeniu nowego skilla

objectives.title: AI Page Annotations - lightweight context for LLM sidebar
objectives.description: Replaced manifest-driven rendering with lightweight AI annotation hooks. Components self-describe via useAIPage/useAIElement, serialized lazily for LLM context. Includes auto-refresh after AI tool execution.

key_results.metric: AI annotation hooks (useAIPage, useAIElement) working
AIPageProvider with ref-based zero-rerender collection
Serializer produces structured LLM context
Scope derivation from annotation endpoints
Key pages annotated (Tasks, Ideas, Decisions, Objectives)
End-to-end wiring: annotations to backend system prompt
Auto-refresh page data after AI tool execution

assumptions: A generic renderer can handle the complexity of current pages without losing UX quality
Manifest definitions are expressive enough to cover all current UI patterns (tables, forms, cards, filters, modals)
Serialized page context fits within LLM token limits without excessive truncation
Incremental migration is possible — manifest pages and traditional pages can coexist
Action execution by AI through manifest dispatch is safe with existing permission system

relations.notes: AI Sidebar Shell is a consumer of manifest system
Enhanced AI Chat will use manifest context for richer interactions

objectives.title: Claude Code Provider — Max subscription integration via official CLI
objectives.description: LLM integration via Anthropic API provider is working. AI Sidebar communicates with LLM, tools execute, auto-refresh works. Claude Code CLI provider (T-176-T-181) was planned but deprioritized — current API provider serves the need. Future: add Claude Max subscription support via LiteLLM or similar.

key_results.metric: ClaudeCodeProvider class implementing LLMProvider protocol
Provider registered in ProviderRegistry with config
Multi-turn conversation via session resume
Error handling: auth, rate limits, timeout, process cleanup
AgentLoop streaming support for non-tool-use providers
Provider tests with mocked subprocess
API key and Max subscription both work as auth methods

assumptions: Claude Code CLI is installed and authenticated with Max subscription on the host machine
Claude Code print mode (-p) and --output-format json/stream-json are stable official features
Claude Code subprocess will NOT be called from within another Claude Code session (web UI only)
Max plan rate limits are sufficient for single-user Forge usage
Session resumption (--resume) works reliably in non-interactive mode

objectives.title: Multi-Session LLM Orchestration — concurrent AI agents with monitoring
objectives.description: Enable multiple concurrent LLM sessions, each executing different work (tasks, planning, verification, compound). User can monitor all active sessions, see what each uses (guidelines, knowledge, objectives), track progress, and navigate to related entities. Sessions can be launched from UI (run tasks, plan objective) or via chat commands.

key_results.metric: Multiple concurrent LLM sessions executing independently
Session dashboard showing all active sessions with status and context
Launch session from UI: run task, plan objective, verify, compound
Session detail view: see messages, tool calls, entities used
Session uses correct guidelines and knowledge per objective/task scopes

objectives.title: Decision Notifications & LLM-Human Handoff — popups, blocking decisions, collaboration
objectives.description: When LLM encounters something requiring human decision during task execution, it creates a decision and the user gets a popup notification. Decision view shows full context: what objective, which task, related guidelines, LLM reasoning. User can navigate to all related entities. Blocking decisions pause LLM execution until resolved.

key_results.metric: LLM creates decision during task execution and user gets popup
Decision detail view with full context links (objective, task, guidelines)
Blocking decisions pause LLM session until user resolves
Notification center showing pending decisions across all sessions

objectives.title: Entity Relationship Views & DAG — linked views, visual task graph, traceability
objectives.description: Rich entity detail views showing full relationship graph: Objective shows its guidelines, research, ideas, linked tasks and their status. Task shows inherited guidelines, assigned knowledge, dependencies. Task dependency DAG visualization. All entities navigable via links. User sees the full picture of WHY something exists and HOW it connects.

key_results.metric: Objective detail shows linked guidelines, research, ideas, tasks with status
Task detail shows inherited guidelines, knowledge, dependency chain
Task DAG visualization with dependency arrows and status colors
Cross-entity navigation: click any linked entity to navigate
Idea detail shows research, decisions, risks, child ideas

objectives.title: LLM Workflow Awareness — AI understands and executes forge process
objectives.description: LLM knows the full forge workflow: objectives, ideas, discovery, planning, execution, verification, compound. Can plan an objective (decompose into tasks with guidelines), execute tasks respecting dependencies, run deep-verify, extract lessons. Understands guideline inheritance (objective scopes to task scopes). Can assign knowledge to tasks during planning. Knows which research/ideas to consider when planning.

key_results.metric: LLM can plan an objective into task graph respecting guidelines and knowledge
LLM executes tasks with correct context (guidelines, knowledge, dependencies)
LLM can run deep-verify on plans and implementations
LLM assigns knowledge and guidelines to tasks during planning
LLM system prompt includes forge workflow description and available operations
